item.title: Were the deaths of a Pennsylvania pastor's two wives coincidence or murder?
item.description: An apparent suicide at a small-town church exposes secrets, betrayal and questions about two more deaths

item.title: Trump threatens Iran and Houthis amid signs of U.S. military buildup
item.description: The U.S. moves more refueling planes to the Mideast and adds medics at a major military hospital as the war with Iran spreads to another key waterway.

item.title: Senate Judiciary Committee prepares to vote on Todd Blanche's nomination for Attorney General
item.description: The Senate Judiciary Committee is set to vote on Todd Blanche's nomination for attorney general on Thursday as bipartisan concerns remain about his recent leadership under President Trump. Taurean Small shares what to expect and dives into the failed Senate effort to direct Mr. Trump to end the Iran war.

item.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must join Abraham Accords to seal nuclear energy deal
item.description: President Trump said Thursday on Truth Social that Saudi Arabia must normalize diplomatic relations with Israel by joining the Abraham Accords as a contingency to the enactment of the nuclear agreement that was signed on Wednesday. Natalie Brand shares the details.

item.title: FDA panel to consider loosening regulations for some peptides
item.description: The FDA panel will review seven peptides and decide if they should be added to a list of substances that can be safely produced by compounding pharmacies.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha moves inland near Texas-Louisiana border
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Vance Boelter gets 2 life terms and more in Minnesota lawmaker shootings
item.description: Vance Boelter, the man who admitted in court to shooting two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses, was sentenced to two life terms in prison followed by 40 years Thursday morning.

item.title: NYPD says dozens rescued from human trafficking during World Cup
item.description: There were 89 people arrested in the New York area over the course of the World Cup, the NYPD said.

item.title: 3 credit card debt relief moves to make this August
item.description: Saddled with high-rate credit card debt now? Here are three credible debt relief moves to consider this August.

item.title: Fewer Americans own homes than thought, new Fed search finds
item.description: Only about 53% of Americans own homes, or about 12 percentage points lower than previous data has shown, the study found.

item.title: Urgent multistate search for woman suspected of killing Florida father
item.description: A multistate search is underway for a woman wanted in connection to a murder investigation. Police say Isabelle Rose Johnson is suspected of killing a Florida father who was found in a shallow grave last month. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Tilly Norwood's creator insists the AI actor is creating Hollywood jobs
item.description: The robot actor is generating work for Hollywood creatives, not stealing jobs from actors, her inventor tells CBS News.

item.title: House again rebukes Trump over Iran, while similar effort stalls in Senate
item.description: The House on Thursday voted to direct President Trump to end the war with Iran, after four Republicans joined Democrats to rebuke the president over his handling of the conflict.

item.title: $10,000 1-year CD vs. $10,000 high-yield savings account: Which will earn more interest?
item.description: Interest earnings with a $10,000 deposit will look similar, but there are big differences savers should know first.

item.title: Senate fails to advance latest attempt to limit Trump's Iran war powers
item.description: The Senate rejected an attempt to rein in President Trump's war powers in Iran, soon after the House adopted a separate measure rebuking the president amid escalations in the conflict in recent days.

item.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must join Abraham Accords as contingency of nuclear deal
item.description: President Trump said Thursday on social media that Saudi Arabia must agree to join the Abraham Accords as a contingency on a U.S.-Saudi Arabia nuclear agreement. In a separate post, he threatened Iran and its Houthi allies with "major military punishment." CBS News' Kathryn Watson has more.

item.title: Secret Service warns of uptick in threats ahead of rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: The Secret Service is warning about a rise in threats ahead of the rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner, which is set for Friday. CBS News' Nicole Sganga has the latest.

item.title: Why the U.S is moving B-1 bombers and refueling jets into Middle East region
item.description: The U.S. is moving B-1 bombers and refueling jets, as well as medical personnel, deeper into the Middle East. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more on what it could mean.

item.title: FDA says new cyclospora outbreak linked to unspecified product
item.description: Federal health officials said they've identified a new outbreak of the cyclospora parasite, but the source hasn't been specified.

item.title: How today's high pet care costs are straining household finances
item.description: Owning a pet now costs thousands of dollars a year, and the financial pressure is mounting for many pet owners.

item.title: Here's how pet ownership costs are changing (and what owners need to do now)
item.description: Pet ownership costs now average thousands of dollars per year, and that's before any medical issues arise.

item.title: How much interest can a $100,000 1-year CD account earn savers now?
item.description: Considering a $100,000 deposit into a 1-year CD account now? Here's how much interest you stand to earn if you do.

item.title: U.S. and Saudis sign nuclear deal, contingent on joining Abraham Accords
item.description: President Trump said on Thursday the deal would include no enrichment of nuclear material.

item.title: Middle East oil producers step up plans to bypass the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As Iran's attacks keep ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz to a trickle and oil prices surge, Gulf states are planning to invest billions in pipeline alternatives.

item.title: Considering tele-vet care for your pet? Here's when it makes sense (and when it doesn't)
item.description: Knowing when a virtual vet visit is the right call for your pet could save you a lot of time — and a lot of money.

item.title: 1.6 million eggs are recalled over potential salmonella contamination
item.description: The recall affects white-shell and cage-free eggs sold under multiple grocery brands in six states. No illnesses have been reported.

item.title: Former model scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in his Paris home
item.description: A former model who accused Jeffrey Epstein associate Daniel Siad of rape says his death in Paris is "very disappointing," as he never faced justice.

item.title: ICE detentions hit highest monthly total in June since crackdown began
item.description: More than 43,000 immigrants were arrested last month as DHS continues to detain people in less-publicized enforcement surges.

item.title: Mexican man killed by truck after ICE encounter was on vacation, officials say
item.description: Juan Jairo Coronilla Durán, 28, died July 14 after authorities said he darted across a busy road in St. Augustine, Florida, and into the path of a truck.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.
